title: ALP SoC Specification v1
description: Machine-readable per-SoC specification consumed by alp-sdk tooling and downstream consumers (alp-studio, etc.). The SDK's per-SoM preset `silicon:` ref (e.g. "alif:ensemble:e3") resolves into one of these files under metadata/socs/<vendor>/<family>/<part>.json.

soc_spec_version.description: Schema version. Bumped on breaking changes.

ref.description: Canonical reference like "alif:ensemble:e3" — used by the SDK loader + downstream consumers to look this file up.

pending_alif_datasheet.description: When true, this file was populated from secondary sources (ALP module datasheet, family pattern). Replace with primary datasheet data before locking metadata for production.

source.description: Filename of the PDF the data was extracted from.

hwrm.description: Optional pointer to a Hardware Reference Manual that supplements the datasheet (Alif Ensemble family uses these heavily). The PDF itself does not live in the repo; this block carries provenance only.

source.description: Filename of the PDF the data was extracted from.

confidential.description: When true, the PDF is NDA-bound and stays out of git. The block is kept here for traceability.

errata.description: Optional vendor errata documents the SoC is subject to. Per-item ids + workarounds let the metadata pipeline surface affected silicon revisions to the studio.

title_quirk.description: Notes about discrepancies between the PDF title and its actual scope.

soc_ram_kb.description: Maximum on-die SRAM in KB across all variants. Per-variant overrides go into variants[].sram_kb.

tcm_kb.description: Maximum tightly-coupled memory in KB (informative; per-core TCM lives in cores[].tcm_kb).

soc_flash_mb.description: Maximum on-die non-volatile memory in MB (MRAM on Alif). Per-variant overrides go into variants[].mram_mb.

always_on_sram_kb.description: Always-on / utility SRAM that survives STOP mode.

peripherals.description: Counts per peripheral kind. Studio uses these to validate which blocks fit on the SoC. Use the SAME keys across vendors so the studio can do generic matching.

capabilities.description: Silicon-determined feature flags. SoM YAMLs may override or extend specific keys via their own capabilities: block (e.g., V2N declares cau: true because the on-module GD32 bridge provides CAU even though the host silicon does not).

inline_aes.description: Inline AES extension on the host core.

packages.description: All packages in which this SoC is offered, regardless of variant. Per-variant package selection lives in variants[].package.

variants.description: Orderable SKUs. Each entry corresponds to one row of the datasheet's Ordering Information table. alp-studio presents these as user-selectable choices when the SKU is configurable.

memory_regions.description: SoC-level fixed memory regions with base addresses.  Used by _resolve_memory_map when the SoM preset has no explicit memory_map: block and the variant sram_banks_kb does not carry base addresses (e.g. Renesas RZ/V2N OCRAM + DDR window).  Entries follow the som-preset memory_map: shape.

notes.description: Freeform extraction notes worth preserving (caveats, page references, ambiguities).

id.description: Canonical core identifier used by board.yaml cores: and SoM preset topology: blocks. Unique within a SoC.

gops.description: Giga ops per second.  Provide either gops or tops (= gops × 1000).

tops.description: Tera ops per second.  Provide either gops or tops.

external.description: true when the accelerator is a separate IC (e.g. DeepX DX-M1).

order_code.description: Vendor part number. For Alif Ensemble this is the 16-character base PN (without -T / -Y shipment suffix).

part_number_root.description: Vendor short name without the package / temperature / qualification suffix (e.g. "i.MX 9352"). Provided when the full order code is awaiting the maintainer's HW-config writeup.

notes.description: Per-variant freeform notes (provenance, pending fields, etc.).

package.description: Package identifier, e.g. WLCSP208, FBGA194. Optional when only the short part-number root is known; promoted to required for production variants once the HW-config writeup lands.

temperature_grade.description: Vendor temperature grade. Maps loosely to operating_temp_c; the canonical numeric range still lives at the SoC level.

sram_banks_kb.description: Per-bank SRAM sizes in KB. Studio uses this for memory-map planning. Bank labels match datasheet (SRAM0, SRAM2 (M55-HP ITCM), etc.).

optional_features.description: Per-variant on/off feature toggles. Common keys: npu_hp, npu_hg, can_fd, isp, jpeg_encoder.

shipment_suffixes.description: Shipment packaging suffixes appended to order_code, e.g. ["-T" (Tape and Reel), "-Y" (Tray)].

alp_module_skus.description: ALP Lab module SKUs (e.g. E1M-AEN701) that ship this Alif variant. Lets the studio resolve E1M SKU → SoC variant in one hop.
